
Object oriented ontology notes [[Category:Pages that existed before the Great November Outage]]
[[sn:Object Oriented Ontology]][[sn:Object Oriented Ontology]]== Theory of everything ==
The four major pitfalls faced a theory of everything are: 

* physicalism
* smallism
* anti-fictionalism
* literalism
Not a materialist worldview (seems to be a neutral monist view)
[[Category:Philosophical movements]]
[[Category:Continental philosophy]]
[[Category:Ontology]]
[[sn:Object Oriented Ontology]][[Category:Epistemology]]
[[Category:Metaphysical theories]]
[[Category:Philosophy of mind]]
[[Category:Perception]]
[[Category:Graham Harman]]
==What is an object==
[[File:Upward vs Downward reduction.svg|thumb|OOO rejects all reduction downwards and upwards]]
* anything that cannot be entirely reduced either to the components of which it is made
* or to the effects that it has on other things.
{{Blockquote|If someone asks us what something is, we might respond 
with millions of different sentences in an attempt to answer 
their question. But ultimately there are just two ways of tell-
ing somebody what a thing is: you can tell them what it is 
made of, or tell them what it does.|OOO page 43}}

== Overmining in the history of philosophy ==
Completely zoned out in this part

== Five Features of Metaphor ==

# Does not give thoughts of perceptions of an object, something in its own right<ref name=":0">OOO page 87</ref>
# Non-reciprocal<ref name=":0" />
# asymmetrical<ref name=":0" />
#Only object is our self<ref name=":0" />
# act of coupling<ref name=":0" />

== References ==

[[Category:Ontology]]
[[sn:Object Oriented Ontology]]
[[Category:Pages]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
